messages.content: 你现在是一个在英语语言大师，帮我完成英语填空题
好的，请给我需要填空的句子和选项。
BT’s prices for national and international calls are now among the lowest in the world, although local call prices are ____ near the cheapest.
A. still B. already C. rarely D. frequently

BT’s prices for national and international calls are now among the lowest in the world, although local call prices are ____ near the cheapest.（1 分）
A．nowhere
B．where
C．anywhere
D．somewhere
A. nowhere

The professor promises that she will have a computer ___ for the whole year.（1 分）
A．
at your disposal
B．
in general
C．
on impulse
D．
up to
A. at your disposal

If you will require childcare in order to attend the meeting, please ____ us by leaving a message for Jan or Martha at Jordan’s office.（1 分）
A．
suffer
B．
supply
C．
notify
D．
terrify
C. notify

The chairman spoke so forcefully that the rest of the committee yielded ______ his opinion.（1 分）
A．
for
B．
down
C．
of
D．
to
D. to

This is to ____ that the holder of this certificate has been awarded top prize in the English-speaking contest.（1 分）
A．
climb
B．
certify
C．
require
D．
contain
B. certify

The interview was really stressful. They were trying to trip me ____ all the way through.（1 分）
A．
after
B．
up
C．
at
D．
on
B. up

The local government has given priority to the construction of infrastructure so as to attract more foreign ______.（1 分）
A．
influence
B．
investment
C．
blood
D．
attack
B. investment

No one noticed that the little boy had ____ into the room and was sitting there, listening.（1 分）
A．
cultivated
B．
announced
C．
adjusted
D．
crept
D. crept

Charlie Chaplin was born in a poor area of South London, but in 1913 he left Britain ______ good.（1 分）
A．
about
B．
again
C．
free
D．
for
D. for

Charlie Chaplin was born in a poor area of South London, but in 1913 he left Britain ______ good.（1 分）
A．
about
B．
again
C．
free
D．
for
D. for

Some poets and philosophers choose to live in solitude because they can find ____ in it.（1 分）
A．
inspiration
B．
ghost
C．
spirit
D．
deck
A. inspiration

As far as I can remember, this was their mother ____. Her voice was strident (刺耳的), and she was ready to be angry at anyone.（1 分）
A．
in earnest
B．
at her best
C．
at her worst
D．
on the way
C. at her worst

The President defended the government policy, _____ the media ____ misinforming the people.（1 分）
A．
succeeding… in
B．
running…of
C．
accusing… of
D．
riding… to
C. accusing… of

The story was so fascinating that she finished reading the book ___.（1 分）
A．
at one sitting
B．
occasionally
C．
sometimes
D．
at times
A. at one sitting

The country is speeding ____ its strategic plan to pump enormous amounts of money into various communications projects in the hope of moving directly into the information age.（1 分）
A．
after
B．
under
C．
up
D．
above
C. up

Not having a good command of English can be a serious ___ preventing you from achieving your goals.（1 分）
A．
fault
B．
obstacle
C．
distress
D．
offense
B. obstacle

It is generally considered unwise to give a child ________ he or she wants.（1 分）
A．
whichever
B．
whatever
C．
whenever
D．
however
B. whatever

The students couldn't help ______ when they heard the joke.（1 分）
A．
laugh
B．
to laughing
C．
to laugh
D．
laughing
D. laughing

The fact ______ he didn't see Lao Li yesterday is true.（1 分）
A．
what
B．
which
C．
that
D．
when
C. that

"_____ your meeting is!" he offered them his sincere congratulations.（1 分）
A．
What great success
B．
How a great success
C．
What a great success
D．
How great success
C. What a great success
下面请根据我给的英文文章来帮我选择选择题，并且输出对应的中文
好的，请给我文章。

Many private institutions of higher education around the country are in danger. Not all will be saved, and perhaps not all deserve to be saved. There are low-quality schools just as there are low-quality business. We have no obligation to save them simply because they exist.
But many thriving institutions that deserve to continue are threatened. They are doing a fine job educationally, but they are caught in a financial squeeze, with no way to reduce rising costs or increase revenues significantly. Raising tuition doesn’t bring in more revenue, for each time tuition goes up, the enrollment goes down, or the amount that must be given away in student aid goes up. Schools are bad businesses, whether public or private, not usually because of mismanagement but because of the nature of the enterprise. They lose money on every customer, and they can go bankrupt either from too few students or too many students. Even a very good college is a very bad business.
It is such colleges, thriving but threatened I worry about. Low enrollment is not their chief problem. Even with full enrollments, they may go under. Efforts to save them, and preferably to keep them private, are a national necessity. There is no basis for arguing that private schools are inherently (固有地) better than public schools. Examples to the contrary abound. Anyone can name state universities and colleges that rank as the finest in the nation and the world. It is now inevitable that public institutions will be dominant, and therefore diversity is a national necessity. Diversity in the way we support schools tends to give us a healthy diversity in the forms of education. In an imperfect society such as ours, uniformity of education throughout the nation could be dangerous. In an imperfect society, diversity is a positive good. Enthusiastic supporters of public higher education know the importance of sustaining private higher education.

1. What is the main concern of the author in this article?
A. The low quality of both private and public institutions of higher education.
B. The financial difficulties faced by many private institutions of higher education.
C. The lack of full enrollment in some private institutions of higher education.
D. The fact that private institutions of higher education are inherently better than public institutions.

答案：B。本文的主要关注点在于讨论当前很多私立高等教育机构面临的财务压力和濒危的问题。

2. What is the nature of colleges according to the author?
A. They are low-quality businesses because of mismanagement.
B. They are bad businesses because they lose money on every customer.
C. They are good businesses because they bring in more revenue in the long run.
D. They are profitable businesses as long as enrollment remains steady.

答案：B。文章中指出，无论是公立还是私立的大学都会因为行业本身的特殊性质导致亏损，因为他们每个客户都会亏本，因此即使学生数量保持稳定，财务状况也仍然会不断恶化。

3. Why does the author believe that efforts to save private institutions of higher education are necessary?
A. Because diversity in the way we support schools tends to give us a healthy diversity in the forms of education.
B. Because private institutions are inherently better than public institutions.
C. Because the government has an obligation to save all private institutions.
D. Because public institutions are not able to provide a diverse enough education. 

答案：A。作者认为，私立高等教育机构面临的困境不能简单地归结为私立机构的本质问题，事实上，这些机构提供了多样化的教育选择，鼓励私立教育提供多样化的教育方式，并保持它们的私营性质是合理的。

According to the author's opinion, schools are bad businesses because of _______.（2 分）
A．
too few students
B．
their characteristics
C．
mismanagement
D．
financial squeeze
B. their characteristics
下面我将给你一篇文章，你将根据我给你的文章内容来帮我解答选择题
好的，请提供文章。

Many private institutions of higher education around the country are in danger. Not all will be saved, and perhaps not all deserve to be saved. There are low-quality schools just as there are low-quality business. We have no obligation to save them simply because they exist.
But many thriving institutions that deserve to continue are threatened. They are doing a fine job educationally, but they are caught in a financial squeeze, with no way to reduce rising costs or increase revenues significantly. Raising tuition doesn’t bring in more revenue, for each time tuition goes up, the enrollment goes down, or the amount that must be given away in student aid goes up. Schools are bad businesses, whether public or private, not usually because of mismanagement but because of the nature of the enterprise. They lose money on every customer, and they can go bankrupt either from too few students or too many students. Even a very good college is a very bad business.
It is such colleges, thriving but threatened I worry about. Low enrollment is not their chief problem. Even with full enrollments, they may go under. Efforts to save them, and preferably to keep them private, are a national necessity. There is no basis for arguing that private schools are inherently (固有地) better than public schools. Examples to the contrary abound. Anyone can name state universities and colleges that rank as the finest in the nation and the world. It is now inevitable that public institutions will be dominant, and therefore diversity is a national necessity. Diversity in the way we support schools tends to give us a healthy diversity in the forms of education. In an imperfect society such as ours, uniformity of education throughout the nation could be dangerous. In an imperfect society, diversity is a positive good. Enthusiastic supporters of public higher education know the importance of sustaining private higher education.

What does the author suggest is a national necessity?
A. Low enrollment in private institutions of higher education.
B. The dominance of public institutions of higher education.
C. Uniformity of education throughout the nation.
D. Diversity in the way that we support schools.

答案：D。最后一段指出，教育多样性带来的好处是显而易见的。在我们这样一个不完美的社会中，全国各地教育的统一性可能是危险的。多样性是一个积极的因素，支持私立高等教育是国家的需要，这也是保持多样性的一种方式。
下面是关于这篇文的选择题
请提供选择题。

According to the author's opinion, schools are bad businesses because of _______.（2 分）
A．
too few students
B．
their characteristics
C．
mismanagement
D．
financial squeeze
B. their characteristics
请告诉我它的中文翻译并且为什么这么选择
题目为“根据作者的观点，学校之所以是不好的生意是因为什么？”。正确答案为 B. 它们的特性。文章中提到，“学校是不好的业务，无论是公立还是私立的，通常不是因为管理不善，而是由于企业的本质。它们每个学生都会亏损，无论是由于学生数量太少还是太多都会破产。即使是非常好的学校也是非常糟糕的生意。”因此，“它们的特性”是正确的答案。

Which of the following is NOT mentioned about private schools?（2 分）
A．
There are many cases to show that public schools are better than private schools.
B．
Private schools are more profitable than public schools.
C．
High-quality private schools deserve to be save
D．
If the tuition of the private schools is raised, the enrollment goes down.
B. Private schools are more profitable than public schools.
请告诉我它的中文翻译并且为什么这么选择

题目为“以下哪项不是提到了私立学校的内容？”正确答案为 B. 私立学校比公立学校更有利润。文章中提到，私立学校并非固有地比公立学校更优秀，许多州立大学和学院是美国和世界最好的。即使是非常好的私立学校可能也会面临濒危的威胁，因为私立学校的捐赠者通常不会超过公立学校捐赠者的数量。文章提到，学校是不好的业务，无论是公立还是私立的，通常不是因为管理不善，而是由于企业的本质，每个学生都会亏损，无论是由于学生数量太少还是太多都会破产。从文章中我们可以看出，文章并没有提到私立学校比公立学校更有利润这一内容。

The author used the phrase “go under" in Para. 3 to mean "_______".（2 分）
A．
have low enrollment
B．
get into difficulties
C．
bring in more money
D．
have little money
B. get into difficulties
请告诉我它的中文翻译并且为什么这么选择

题目为“作者在第3段使用了“go under”这一短语，意思是"_______"。”正确答案为 B. 遇到困难。文章中提到，即使是非常好的学院也是非常糟糕的生意，并且面临消失的风险，“低入学率不是他们的主要问题，即使在全部入学的情况下，他们可能也会面临濒危的风险”。文章没有提及“have low enrollment”“bring in more money”和“have little money”的意思，因此选项B最准确。

We can reasonably conclude from this passage that the author made an appeal to the public in order to support_____.（2 分）
A．
private schools
B．
public institution
C．
diversity of education
D．
uniformity of education
C. diversity of education
请告诉我它的中文翻译并且为什么这么选择
题目为“我们可以合理地得出结论，作者在撰写这篇文章时呼吁公众支持_____。”正确答案为C.教育多样性。文章中提到，在这个不完美的社会中，全国各地教育的统一性可能是危险的，多样性是一个积极的因素，支持私立高等教育是国家的需要，这也是保持多样性的一种方式。文章中明确提到了支持多样性的观点，因此选项C是正确的答案。
